status: synthetic validation-design reference; not external material validation

source_operator.citation: Chang et al., Applied Physics Letters 89, 062109 (2006) and Journal of Electronic Materials 36, 1000-1006 (2007)

design_decision.optimum_rule: For fixed absolute cutoff and relative thickness uncertainties, relative W uncertainty decreases monotonically with abs(ln(d2/d1)); choose the largest ratio that preserves the tail branch, source domain, and declared energy margin.
design_decision.reason: The ratio 4 pair uses 92.1 percent of the maximum ratio available with a 2 meV source-domain margin and leaves 2.994 meV above the lower source bound.
design_decision.minimum_precision_guidance: Independent per-cutoff uncertainty at or below approximately 1.18 meV supports 10 percent W precision when the effective-thickness ratio is exact; 2 percent independent thickness uncertainties lower the cutoff-error budget to approximately 1.15 meV.

authorized_conclusions: Two same-state tail cutoffs identify W directly through their energy difference and logarithmic effective-thickness ratio.
Larger thickness ratios improve W conditioning until a crossing leaves the tail branch or authorized energy domain.
The synthetic 5-to-20 um pair is close to the largest informative ratio that retains a 2 meV source-domain margin.
Correlated energy-calibration errors can substantially reduce paired-difference uncertainty and must be retained explicitly.
Effective-thickness uncertainty contributes separately from cutoff-energy uncertainty.
unauthorized_conclusions: The record is not a fit to Chang Figure 2.
The optimum ratio is not universal outside the declared W, baseline cutoff, response criterion, and source domain.
Physical thickness is not treated as effective optical thickness.
No external material validation is complete.
